title: Fix 403 error when analyzing mobile screenshots

requirements.summary: Fix HTTP 403 error for mobile screenshot uploads by reviewing API request format, ensuring proper image encoding, headers, and authentication
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile screenshots can be analyzed without returning 403 errors
Error handling provides clear Portuguese feedback when API authentication fails
API requests include all required headers and authentication tokens for the Google Gemini Vision endpoint

file_operations.description: Review and fix the analyzeChart function to ensure mobile screenshots are properly encoded as base64 data URIs with correct MIME types. Verify that the Google Gemini Vision API request includes all required headers (Content-Type: application/json) and properly formatted authentication in the URL query parameter. Ensure the image data is sent in the correct format expected by Gemini's REST API (inline data with mime_type and data fields). Add validation to check that base64 encoding is complete and valid before sending the request.

Review the GEMINI endpoint configuration to ensure the API key placeholder and URL format match Google's current Gemini Vision API requirements. Verify that the endpoint URL structure supports the generateContent method with proper versioning. Add comments documenting the required API key format and any specific Google Cloud project permissions needed for the Gemini Vision API.

requirements.summary: Add diagnostic logging to capture request details when API calls fail with 403 status
acceptanceCriteria: Console logs display request metadata when 403 errors occur
Error messages distinguish between authentication failures and other API errors
Logs help identify differences between desktop and mobile image upload requests

file_operations.description: Add comprehensive console logging before fetch requests to capture: request URL (with API key redacted), request headers, payload size in bytes, image MIME type, and base64 data length. When a 403 error occurs, log the full error response body, response headers, and request metadata to help diagnose authentication issues. Add a helper function to safely log request details without exposing sensitive API keys. Include device/platform detection (mobile vs desktop) in error logs to identify mobile-specific issues.

Update error handling in the handleAnalysis function to display more specific Portuguese error messages when a 403 status is received. Distinguish between 'Erro de autenticação da API' (authentication error), 'Chave de API inválida' (invalid API key), and 'Permissões insuficientes' (insufficient permissions). Log the image source (camera, upload, or clipboard) along with device type when analysis fails to help correlate 403 errors with mobile uploads.

requirements.summary: Validate Google Gemini Vision API key configuration and permissions before sending requests
acceptanceCriteria: API key validation prevents requests when credentials are missing or invalid
User receives clear Portuguese error message ('Chave de API não configurada') if API key is missing
Configuration documentation includes steps to verify API key permissions

file_operations.description: Add validateApiKey helper function that checks if the Gemini API key is present, non-empty, and not a placeholder value (like 'YOUR_API_KEY_HERE'). Call this validation at the start of analyzeChart before making the HTTP request. Throw a descriptive error with Portuguese message 'Chave de API não configurada. Configure a chave no arquivo apiConfig.ts' when validation fails. Add comments documenting required Google Cloud API permissions (Generative Language API enabled, unrestricted API key or proper HTTP referrer restrictions).

Add inline documentation comments above the GEMINI configuration explaining: 1) How to obtain a Gemini API key from Google AI Studio, 2) Required API enablement in Google Cloud Console (Generative Language API), 3) Recommended API key restrictions (HTTP referrers for production), 4) How to verify the API key has proper permissions by testing with curl, 5) Common 403 error causes (quota exceeded, API not enabled, referrer restrictions). Add a TODO comment reminding developers to replace the placeholder API key.

requirements.summary: Ensure mobile screenshots are properly preprocessed (format conversion, resizing) before sending to Google Gemini Vision API
acceptanceCriteria: Images larger than API limits are automatically resized while preserving aspect ratio
HEIC/HEIF formats from iOS devices are converted to JPEG before upload
Image preprocessing maintains sufficient quality for chart analysis

file_operations.description: Add preprocessImage async helper function that: 1) Creates an Image object from the file/blob, 2) Checks dimensions against Gemini Vision limits (max 4096x4096 pixels), 3) If oversized, creates a canvas and draws the image scaled down proportionally to fit within limits, 4) Converts canvas to JPEG blob with 0.9 quality using canvas.toBlob, 5) Returns the processed File object. Call preprocessImage on the input file before converting to base64 in analyzeChart. Add comments documenting Gemini's image size limits and supported formats (JPEG, PNG, WebP, but not HEIC). For HEIC detection, check file.type === 'image/heic' or file.name.endsWith('.heic') and show a Portuguese error message instructing users to convert or use camera to capture as JPEG instead.

Update the file validation logic to detect HEIC/HEIF formats and show a warning message in Portuguese: 'Arquivos HEIC não são suportados. Por favor, converta para JPEG ou use a câmera.' Add accept attribute to the file input restricting to 'image/jpeg,image/png,image/webp' to guide users toward supported formats. Update MAX_FILE_SIZE comment to mention that images will be automatically resized if they exceed Gemini's limits.

Add a useEffect hook that detects the image source (file upload, camera, clipboard) and logs it to sessionStorage alongside the imageData. This metadata will help correlate preprocessing with different input methods. When storing imageData from camera or clipboard, add a comment noting that these sources typically provide JPEG format which is already compatible, while file uploads may need preprocessing.
